prompts: The carbon cycle in an ecosystem involves carbon dioxide being absorbed by plants during photosynthesis, converting it into organic matter. Animals and other organisms consume these plants, incorporating carbon into their bodies. Respiration by plants, animals, and microorganisms releases carbon dioxide back into the atmosphere. When organisms die, decomposers break down their bodies, further releasing carbon into the atmosphere and soil. This cycle is balanced by carbon storage in the ocean, soil, and fossil fuels, where it can be sequestered for long periods, contributing to the Earth's long-term carbon storage and exchange system.
